title: Bandais Rebuild model kits
wikitext:
[]: Bandai's Rebuild model kits             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Wed Feb 03, 2010 12:04 pm After years of waiting without even a peep out of Bandai....this news is disappointing http://ngeekhiong.blogspot.com/2010/02/new-evangelion-plamo-gurren-lagann.html The first new Bandai kits (Eva-01 and 00) came out back in August 23rd 2007, a good 2 weeks or so before the first movie hit theatres. Since then Bandai has been regularly popping them out every few months along with the old TV/EoE kits, but there was absolutely no news of anything from 2.0 until now, so later after release. And now all we get is typical Bandai behaviour, a recolor or an existing kit when an equal amount of effort could have poped out some new models :\ I just hope this means they're still interested in keeping the model line (yes they did actually start numbering them so many moons ago) going and I'll finally get my Rebuild Eva-02. I also hope that by the time Q/Final roll around they'll be popping new Evas out like mad. They could milk this in a really awesome way but yet they dont :( ----------------------------EDIT---------------------------- Thread title changed to be more general instead of complain-y

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Wed Feb 03, 2010 12:47 pm I think Bandai will abandon the "Model Kit" market for Evangelion and instead invest time and money into the action figure market. I mean why else are they investing their efforts in the HCM Pro line to coincide with the release of Evangelion 1.0? And now with Evangelion 2.0, they are in intent on churning out Eva Robot Damashii action figures. Evas are definitely no Gundam and deserve better than limited and clunky Bandai Model kits.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Wed Feb 03, 2010 12:57 pm The bandai kits are neither limited nor clunky. Toys are for pussies anyway. Real men build and paint their favorite cartoon fetish objects anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Wed Feb 03, 2010 1:10 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Toys are for pussies anyway. Real men build and paint their favorite cartoon fetish objects anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Wed Feb 03, 2010 2:15 pm They are COLLECTIBLES not toy ! -it's okay sweetheart he didnt mean it. *pets his revoltech eva-02* btw, it looks like the HCM pro line, not a kit even the price tag is in the same radious If they had done a eva 02, with the hcm line, i would have bought it (Even though is disliked the purple used on unit 01, but now my damashis are preoordered so i dont care much for eva 00.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Wed Feb 03, 2010 3:52 pm I have the Rebuild Eva 00 model and I was not happy with the result (due to some issues with the segmented torso). It's pretty clear that Bandai didn't put the same kind of effort into their secondary licenses (such as the Rebuild Eva models) as they do with even High Grade Gundam models. Are the older Evangelion models any better? On the other hand, the HCM pro figures are among the best Eva figures I've ever had (they're only beaten by the Xebec figures).

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Wed Feb 03, 2010 4:54 pm Holy Diver wrote: It's pretty clear that Bandai didn't put the same kind of effort into their secondary licenses (such as the Rebuild Eva models) as they do with even High Grade Gundam models. Are the older Evangelion models any better? On the other hand, the HCM pro figures are among the best Eva figures I've ever had (they're only beaten by the Xebec figures).

[]:             Quote Post by chigoki » Mon Mar 15, 2010 5:08 am yep the older eva model kits have sturdier joints in the torso, i have the rebuild version of unit-01 and his hands and upper torso keeps on popping out, i can never get shogoki hold that big ass gattling gun

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Apr 01, 2010 10:02 am So the new Eva-01 Awakened Ver. finally came out. I have to say, it is so much more awesome than I expected. You get most of the old kit with quite a few parts unused. Like with the old kits this really comes in handy sometimes. For this new one you not only can build the Awakened Ver., but also get all the parts need to build a plain, updated Eva-01. Changes: http://www.mresources.info/hobby/rebuild_updated_eva-01.jpg -lower torso armor resculpted -neck resculpted -both old head and new Awakened raging head included -outer parts of the upper shoulder updated to go along with the slight redesign between the movies, see lineart -thighs resculpted -added effects parts Pics at hobbysearch: http://www.1999.co.jp/eng/10108846 I really hope they hurry up with the new Eva-02. The movie comes out on DVD and BD in Japan on May 26th, they could easily pop it out in time. ------------EDIT-------------- It looks like my prayers have been answered May release: * Eva Unit 1 New Theatrical Ver. - 3,500 Yen (exclusive of tax). June release: * Eva Unit 2 New Theatrical Ver. - 4,800 Yen (exclusive of tax). The yet another Eva-01 is probably just a normal color updated version without the gimmicks which is why the price is lower. On the other hand Eva-02 is much more expensive, probably due to the backpack and the tons of guns.

[]:             Quote Post by chigoki » Mon Apr 12, 2010 6:10 am finally a rebuild version of unit-02 model kit

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri May 14, 2010 9:29 am Alrighty then. My Awakened Ver. Eva-01 arrived on tuesday, I snapped it wednesday and been taking a few pics. So as previously posted lineart shows there's quite a few changes between 1.0 and 2.0 Eva-01 and the Dengeki page shows most of them, but it's still fun to do a direct comparison. The neck has been shortened and reshaped: http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/necks.jpg The normal and the super angry Awakened head parts: http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/heads.jpg Other than that to me the biggest and most awesome change is the torso sculpt. It always looked a little iffy to me on the original Rebuild kit, didn't really look like the armor fit together nicely and was a little skinny. The new Rebuild 2.0 Eva torso sculpt is much lovlier. Not side by side comparisons this time because it's more fun to see the changes by opening up the pics in different tabs and flipping between the two versions. Some of these also show the different sculpt of the thighs but the difference really isn't nearly as noticable. http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/torso_01.jpg http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/torso_02.jpg http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/torso_03.jpg http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/torso_04.jpg http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/torso_05.jpg http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/torso_06.jpg http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/torso_07.jpg http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/torso_08.jpg http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/torso_09.jpg http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/torso_10.jpg http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/torso_11.jpg http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/torso_12.jpg Then another really obvious change is the arms (included original TV kit just for fun): http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/arms.jpg New and old parts: http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/trees_01.jpg That last pic is probably the most interesting to me because with it I finally understand how the hell bandai splits up their molds into both block for design variation and colors. That little defect gives it away, it seems on each side of the mold they have a twistable little part to direct the flow where they want when they want some variation. This new tree also shows that it's been made with Eva-02 in mind. Unlike 00, 01 amd 03, the upped shoulder on 02 are a different color than most of the armor: http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/05.jpg http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/trees_02.jpg Overall the Awakened Ver. kit is very fun, both flavors of the orange parts even glow unter UV light http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/glow.jpg So far in this new lineup of Eva kits we have: 01 - Eva-01 Rebuild 1.0 02 - Eva-00 Rebuild 1.0 03 - Eva-01 Rebuild 2.0 Awakened 04 - Eva-01 Rebuild 2.0 Vanilla 05 - Eva-02 Rebuild 2.0 With the way the new molds are made Bandai could very easily pop out with little effort: Eva-00 Rebuild 2.0 Eva-03 Eva-04 (like more than a decade ago, chrome the 03 and add the shield from the Pachinko game) With a little more effort they could do Eva-05, but that might end up almost a 6000 yen kit probably. That's going from Eva-02 being 4500 from only a few extra guns and a jetpack. The massive 4-legged Eva-05 would easily top 5000 for sure. Some non-spoilerific lineart for 2.0: http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/04.jpg http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/05.jpg http://www.mresources.info/hobby/Shogouki/06.jpg Also I'm not quite sure if I've mentioned this earlier, but 2.0 also has Eva-06 which is another simple humanoid. That could be another simple kit for Bandai to pop-out, but I don't really remember that many design details of it other than a visor head. Finally from the trailer for 3.0 we know there is an Eva-08, which also implies and Eva-07 somewhere in the future. Still tons of kits to do, but the wait could be loooooooooooooong. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ May 14th EDIT ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ Meanwhile, at the 49th Shizuoka Hobby Show in Japan right now: Awakened Eva-01 = already out, 3,800 yen Normal 2.0 Eva-01 = May 28th, 3,800 yen Rebuild Eva-02 (June 24th - 4,800 yen) 2.0 Pylon'd Eva-00 (sample display, no date or price yet)

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Fri May 14, 2010 9:38 am Nice model kits. Though i still dislike the Unit 01's torso. Looks to tv series-ish to me and not necessarily as faithful to the Rebuild movies as i would want them to be. Also quick question, but will you painting your Eva model kits to add the finishing details? If so which paints will you use?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri May 14, 2010 10:36 am Whenever I do get around to painting my kits they'll be airbrushed with Mr. Color paints. I'm hoping to have one of ye olde TV Eva-02 kits done by the time the Rebuild kit comes out in June.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Wed Jun 23, 2010 7:54 pm Does *anyone* has the model of unit 00 (the one from Rebuild 1.0)?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Jun 24, 2010 10:45 am arael wrote: Does *anyone* has the model of unit 00 (the one from Rebuild 1.0)?

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Thu Jun 24, 2010 12:13 pm TMBounty_Hunter.- Good! Tell me: I've seen many reviews on youtube and some online shopping stores, but none is very specific about certain stuff, can you please tell me: -How tall the figure is fully assembled? -It comes with a Lance of Longinus right? If so, how long is it (in inches)? -What other accesory comes with the figure? -Does it have stand or base? -If a train departs from New York at 3:00 pm directed to Washington, travelling at 70 miles per hour, at what time will it arrive?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Jun 24, 2010 9:19 pm -about 21cm (really depends how you post it, spread and bent legs, bet torso, etc) -yes, they recycled the awesome huge 4-piece lance from the 2nd old MP Eva kit. it's 36.3cm long -it's all listed on the box. websites like Hobby Search have scans of the box/manuals and pics of the kit and show you all you get -see box (shorter answer: no) -the train wouldn't travel at 70mph but at about 310mph because while everyone is distracted by the oil spill Obama build a bullet train network connecting all the major cities on the east coast

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Thu Jun 24, 2010 9:45 pm Yes! I'm totally buying it! 36.6 cm is huge! Revoltech's is only about 30 and its almost -flat- (yes... I'm buying it for the lance only ;D) -I knew Obama was up to something! Any info of the same bizarre sort on the Bandai model kit F-type Unit 1; and release date for the 2.0 unit 00? (yes, buying it only for the missile)

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Jun 25, 2010 1:02 am arael wrote: Any info of the same bizarre sort on the Bandai model kit F-type Unit 1;

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sun Jun 27, 2010 1:14 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: The Side-Eva toy is pretty much as complete a package as you can get if you want the 2.0 Eva-00. Bandages and missle included.
